text: Agile methodology is a modern approach to project management, especially common in software development. Work is divided into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting one to four weeks. Each sprint begins by selecting tasks from the backlog, a list of all the work needed. These tasks are described as user stories, which explain features from the user's perspective. Every morning, the team holds a standup, a quick meeting where members discuss progress and any problems. Agile uses iterative development, meaning teams build and improve the product step by step. After each sprint, the team reviews the results and adjusts plans for the next sprint, allowing for flexibility and continuous improvement.
